forecasts.reasoning: Sadr has a well-established pattern of announcing political boycotts and retirements only to return when conditions are more favorable. Although he withdrew his bloc from Parliament in 2022 and boycotted the 2023 provincial elections (and has reportedly reaffirmed this boycott at times, as referenced in the background), his movement's rebranding to the "National Shi'ite Movement" in April 2024 strongly signals preparation for a political comeback ahead of the 2025 parliamentary elections. For an October 2025 election, the Independent High Electoral Commission (IHEC) registration deadlines would typically fall between May and July 2025, forcing his party to make its intentions public (either via an outright announcement or by officially registering candidate lists) prior to the July 1, 2025 deadline specified in the query. While there remains a risk that Sadr might decide to boycott or that the elections are delayed beyond 2025 (in which case an announcement might be deferred), political imperatives and recent mobilizations lean towards his participation. Taking into account his unpredictability and historical precedent, I estimate a 63% chance.
